text:
Centre for Epilepsy

Neurosciences Division 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Patel  
Riverside Medical Practice  
21 Meadow Lane, Oxford OX1 3QF  

Dear Dr Patel,                                                                                                         

Amelia Turner, DOB: 22-11-1991, Hospital No: H382914 NHS No. 9482105736  
Flat 3, 8 Willow Court, Oxford, OX2 7DN  

Epilepsy 

Medication: Continuing Levetiracetam since March 2024  

Levetiracetam 1000 mg twice daily (patient confirms adherence) 

Levetiracetam Blood Level: 

12 September 2025: within therapeutic range (laboratory reference available on file). Dose 1000 mg twice daily  

Seizures: Patient reports no events, warnings, or auras for over 18 months; clinical picture suggests seizure control maintained without recent breakthrough. 

I conducted a video review today. She reports full daytime functioning, no medication side effects, and no missed doses. No safety concerns raised. Plan: continue current regimen; routine follow‑up in 6 months or sooner if any recurrence. 

Yours sincerely

entities.text: no events, warnings, or auras for over 18 months

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states: "Patient reports no events, warnings, or auras for over 18 months," indicating sustained absence of seizures. This exceeds the 6‑month threshold for considering the patient seizure free. No other seizure frequencies are mentioned. Therefore, normalize to a seizure-free duration using the closest explicit numeric value.
seizure_frequency_number: no events, warnings, or auras for over 18 months

flaws.description: Gold (seizure free for 18 month) disagrees with Reference (seizure free for multiple month)
